messages.content: Return only valid JSON that matches the requested schema. Do not include markdown or explanations.

Extraction-first architecture is mandatory.
Complete stages strictly in this order:
Stage 1: Structure Extraction
Stage 2: Competency Extraction
Stage 3: Assessment Extraction
Stage 4: Learning Outcomes Extraction
Stage 5: Activities / Projects / Practicals Extraction
Stage 6: Curriculum Intelligence Generation

Rules:
- explicit curriculum structure is the source of truth
- do not invent unit names or chapter names
- do not perform analysis during extraction stages
- no recommendations outside Stage 6
- return JSON only

Stage 6: Curriculum Intelligence Generation
- You may now perform inference.
- Use only Stage 1 structural entities as anchors.
- Do not create new unit names or chapter names.
- Generate only: dependency graph, lesson planning intelligence, difficulty estimation, concept density, teaching hours.

Stage 1 structure:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Mathematics",
    "class": "IX",
    "grade": "",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "2026 – 27",
    "total_pages": "13"
  },
  "units": [
    {
      "unit_id": "U1",
      "unit_name": "Number System",
      "chapters": [
        {
          "chapter_id": "C1",
          "chapter_name": "Number System",
          "topics": [
            "Introduction to rational numbers",
            "Representation of rational numbers on the number line",
            "Density of rational numbers and its proof",
            "Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers",
            "Decimal representation of rational numbers",
            "Introduction to irrational numbers",
            "Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3",
            "The square root spiral"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_id": "U2",
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapters": [
        {
          "chapter_id": "C1",
          "chapter_name": "Introduction to Polynomials",
          "topics": [
            "Algebraic expressions",
            "Definition of a polynomial.",
            "Degree of a polynomial",
            "Introduction to linear polynomials and applications",
            "Exploring linear patterns",
            "Modelling linear growth and linear decay",
            "Linear relationships",
            "Visualising linear relationships",
            "Slope and y-intercept of a line y = ax + b"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C2",
          "chapter_name": "Sequences and Progressions",
          "topics": [
            "Introduction to sequences",
            "Explicit or general rule of a sequence",
            "Recursive rule of a sequence",
            "Arithmetic Progressions (AP): nth term, visualising an AP, and practical contexts leading to Aps",
            "Sum of the first n natural numbers",
            "Geometric Progressions (GP): nth term, visualising a GP, and practical contexts leading to GPs",
            "Applications of GP in fractals",
            "Tower of Hanoi puzzle"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C3",
          "chapter_name": "Exploring Algebraic Identities",
          "topics": [
            "Revisiting algebraic identities",
            "Visualising identities using geometrical models",
            "Factorisation of algebraic expressions using identities",
            "More identities and their applications",
            "Visualising factorisation of quadratic expressions through algebra tiles and without using algebra tiles",
            "Finding new identities",
            "Simplifying rational expressions"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C4",
          "chapter_name": "Linear Equations in Two Variables",
          "topics": [
            "Introduction to linear equations in two variables through practical examples",
            "Solution of linear equation in two variables: graphical representation",
            "Slope-intercept form of linear equation in two variables",
            "Drawing graphs of linear equations when x and y assume only certain values",
            "Pair of linear equations in two variables",
            "Graphical method for solving a pair of linear equations in two variables",
            "Nature of solutions: consistency and inconsistency",
            "Algebraic methods of solving a pair of linear equations: substitution and elimination method"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_id": "U3",
      "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "chapters": [
        {
          "chapter_id": "C1",
          "chapter_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
          "topics": [
            "Brief history of coordinate geometry",
            "The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system",
            "Distance between two points in the 2-D plane",
            "Midpoint of the line- segment between two points in the 2-D plane"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_id": "U4",
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapters": [
        {
          "chapter_id": "C1",
          "chapter_name": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates",
          "topics": [
            "History of geometry",
            "Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras",
            "Discovering Euclid's definitions",
            "Axioms: Axioms of measurement and rules for geometric objects"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C2",
          "chapter_name": "Lines and Angles",
          "topics": [
            "Rays and angles",
            "Measures of angles",
            "Intersecting lines and angles",
            "Pairs of angles",
            "Theorems and examples on intersecting lines",
            "Theorems and examples on parallel lines"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C3",
          "chapter_name": "Triangles – Congruence Theorems",
          "topics": [
            "Practical applications of triangles",
            "Proofs of conditions of congruence of triangles",
            "Theorems on triangles",
            "Propositions and their converse",
            "Problems based on applications of theorems on triangles"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C4",
          "chapter_name": "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)",
          "topics": [
            "Properties of parallelograms",
            "Important theorems related to parallelograms and their proof",
            "Midpoint theorem and its applications",
            "Understanding the notion of central symmetry in the context of parallelograms"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C5",
          "chapter_name": "Circles",
          "topics": [
            "Practical applications and uses of circles",
            "Definitions related to a circle — centre, diameter, and radius",
            "Chords and the angles they subtend",
            "Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors of chords",
            "Distance of chords from the centre",
            "Subtended angles by an arc",
            "Cyclicity of points"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_id": "U5",
      "unit_name": "Mensuration",
      "chapters": [
        {
          "chapter_id": "C1",
          "chapter_name": "Area and Perimeter",
          "topics": [
            "Perimeter of shapes",
            "Perimeter of a circle: Introduction to Pi and its irrationality",
            "Length of an arc",
            "Area of shapes: rectangles, parallelograms, and triangles",
            "Heron's formula",
            "Squaring a rectangle: Proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras",
            "Area of a circle: derivation",
            "Area of the sector of a circle",
            "Brahmagupta's formula for area of a cyclic 4-gon",
            "Heron's formula as a special case of Brahmagupta's formula"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C2",
          "chapter_name": "Surface Area and Volume",
          "topics": [
            "Surface areas and volumes of spheres (including hemispheres) and right circular cones",
            "Compute the surface area and volume of a cuboid.",
            "Describe a right circular cylinder using its radius and height.",
            "Recognise cones in daily life, and describe them using radius and height.",
            "Recognise a pyramid, and identify its base and apex."
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_id": "U6",
      "unit_name": "Statistics and Probability",
      "chapters": [
        {
          "chapter_id": "C1",
          "chapter_name": "Statistics",
          "topics": [
            "Graphical representation of data",
            "Measures of central tendency"
          ]
        },
        {
          "chapter_id": "C2",
          "chapter_name": "Introduction to Probability",
          "topics": [
            "Concept of probability and randomness",
            "The probability scale",
            "Empirical probability: analysing statistical data and performing experiments",
            "Theoretical probability: sample space and events",
            "Representing probability through tree diagrams and tables"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Stage 2 competencies:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "C-1.1",
      "title": "Understanding of Numbers and Real Number Properties",
      "description": "Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the concept of a rational number.",
        "Represent rational numbers on the number line.",
        "Understand the properties of rational numbers.",
        "Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.",
        "Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.",
        "Understand the concept of irrational numbers.",
        "Prove the irrationality of √2 and √3."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Number System",
      "chapter_name": "Number System"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-1.1, C-9.x",
      "title": "Computational Thinking in Number Systems",
      "description": "Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.",
      "competencies": [
        "Generate decimal expansions systematically using step-by-step logical procedures."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Number System",
      "chapter_name": "Number System"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-3.1, C-3.2, CG-9",
      "title": "Algebraic Modelling and Problem Solving",
      "description": "Models real-life situations in the form of equations to solve them.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.",
        "Define a polynomial and identify its degree, terms, and coefficients.",
        "Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.",
        "Explain and identify patterns in linear relationships."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Introduction to Polynomials"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-3.2, CG-9",
      "title": "Linear Equations Modelling and Graphing",
      "description": "Models and solves contextualised problems using equations.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation in two variables.",
        "Graph a linear equation in two variables."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Introduction to Polynomials"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-3.2, C-8.1, CG-9",
      "title": "Linear Equations in Two Variables: Solving and Modelling",
      "description": "Solve contextualised problems using pairs of linear equations.",
      "competencies": [
        "Graph a pair of linear equations.",
        "Solve a pair of linear equations graphically, by substitution, or elimination.",
        "Determine the nature of solutions (consistency/inconsistency).",
        "Model and solve contextualised problems using pairs of linear equations."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-8.1, CG-9",
      "title": "Sequences and Progressions Modelling",
      "description": "Models patterns using sequences and progressions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the concept of a sequence of numbers.",
        "Identify patterns in sequences and predict terms.",
        "Determine recursive and explicit rules for different sequences.",
        "Obtain terms given recursive or explicit rules."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-8.1, CG-9",
      "title": "Arithmetic and Geometric Progressions Analysis",
      "description": "Analyse attributes of APs and GPs.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP) and determine the nth term.",
        "Visualise an AP graphically.",
        "Identify Geometric Progressions (GP) and determine the nth term.",
        "Visualise a GP graphically."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-8.1, CG-9",
      "title": "Advanced Sequence Applications",
      "description": "Apply sequences to fractals and puzzles.",
      "competencies": [
        "Analyse attributes of fractals using GP.",
        "Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.2, CG-9",
      "title": "Visualising Algebraic Identities",
      "description": "Use computational thinking to visualise algebraic identities.",
      "competencies": [
        "Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Exploring Algebraic Identities"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.2, CG-9",
      "title": "Factorisation and Simplification Strategies",
      "description": "Use computational thinking strategies for factorisation.",
      "competencies": [
        "Determine factors of algebraic expressions using identities.",
        "Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models (with/without tiles).",
        "Find simplified versions of rational expressions."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Exploring Algebraic Identities"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-4.5, CG-9",
      "title": "Coordinate Geometry and Spatial Reasoning",
      "description": "Specify locations and describe spatial relationships using coordinates.",
      "competencies": [
        "Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.",
        "Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.",
        "Determine whether three points lie in a straight line.",
        "Compute the position of the midpoint of a line segment."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Coordinate Geometry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-4.5, CG-9",
      "title": "Geometric Verification via Coordinates",
      "description": "Apply computational thinking to verify geometric properties.",
      "competencies": [

      ],
      "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Coordinate Geometry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.1, C-7.3",
      "title": "Euclid's Axioms and Constructions",
      "description": "Proves mathematical statements using axioms and postulates.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe the history of geometry (India, Egypt, Greece).",
        "Understand definitions, axioms, and postulates.",
        "State Euclid's 5 postulates.",
        "Define parallelism.",

      "chapter_name": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates"

      "title": "Lines and Angles Reasoning",
      "description": "Apply computational thinking to analyse geometric ideas.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain notions of angle and ray.",
        "Classify angles (acute, right, obtuse, reflex).",
        "State and apply linear pair theorem and converse.",
        "Follow proof by contradiction in geometry."

      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Lines and Angles"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.1, C-7.3, CG-9",
      "title": "Theorems on Intersecting and Parallel Lines",
      "description": "Justify geometric results through logical reasoning.",
      "competencies": [
        "Prove vertically opposite angles are equal.",
        "Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles.",
        "Explain transitivity of parallelism."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Lines and Angles"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.1, C-7.3, CG-9",
      "title": "Triangle Properties and Rigidity",
      "description": "Explain triangle rigidity and congruence.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain that a triangle is rigid unlike a quadrilateral.",

      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Triangles – Congruence Theorems"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.1, C-7.3, CG-9",
      "title": "Congruence Conditions and Proofs",
      "description": "Use congruence axioms to prove properties.",
      "competencies": [

      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Triangles – Congruence Theorems"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.1, C-7.3, CG-9",
      "title": "Propositions and Converse Reasoning",
      "description": "Explain propositions and their converses.",
      "competencies": [
        "Use counter examples to show that some converses are false.",

      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Triangles – Congruence Theorems"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-4.2, C-7.3",
      "title": "Quadrilaterals and Parallelograms",

      "competencies": [

      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-4.2, C-7.3",
      "title": "Midpoint Theorem and Symmetry",
      "description": "Prove midpoint theorem and understand symmetry.",
      "competencies": [
        "Prove the midpoint theorem and its converse.",
        "Understand reflection and rotation symmetries of 4-gons."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.3, CG-9",
      "title": "Circle Definitions and Properties",
      "description": "State definitions and explain circle properties.",
      "competencies": [

      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Circles"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.3, CG-9",
      "title": "Circle Theorems on Chords and Angles",
      "description": "Explain relationships between chords, arcs, and angles.",
      "competencies": [

      "chapter_name": "Circles"

Stage 3 assessment:
{
  "assessment_framework": {

    ],

      {
        "level_1": "Remembering: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts.",
        "level_2": "Understanding: Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting."
      },
      {
        "level_3": "Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way."
      },
      {
        "level_4": "Analysing: Examine and break information into parts.",
        "level_5": "Evaluating: Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information or quality of work.",
        "level_6": "Creating: Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern."
      }
    ],
    "internal_assessment": [
      {
        "component": "Pen Paper Test",
        "marks": 5,
        "description": "Multiple Assessment"
      },
      {
        "component": "Portfolio",
        "marks": 5,
        "description": "Continuous assessment of work samples and projects."
      }
    ],
    "practical_assessment": [

        "component": "Lab Practical",
        "marks": 5,
        "description": "Activities to be done from prescribed books (Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools)."
      },
      {
        "prescribed_books": [
          "Mathematics - Textbook for class IX - NCERT Publication",
          "Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX - CBSE Publication",
          "Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage - NCERT Publication",
          "Mathematics exemplar problems for class IX, NCERT publication"
        ]
      }
    ],
    "project_assessment": [
      {
        "component": "Portfolio (Project Work)",
        "marks": 5,
        "description": "Integrated into Internal Assessment as a portfolio component."
      },
      {
        "note": "Continuous and holistic assessment through projects forms an integral part of the learning process."
      }
    ]
  }
}

{
  "learning_outcomes": [

      "unit_name": "Number System",
